title: lonchocarpus lanceolatus
intro: Lonchocarpus lanceolatus, also known as the lanceleaf or velvet bean tree, is a tropical plant native to Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The seeds of the plant contain a compound called rotenone, which has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Lonchocarpus lanceolatus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support overall well-being.
